item_spec.concrete_change:
Implement the declared-but-unimplemented queryLimits method on the existing providers as a documented, best-effort, never-throwing operation, and make the per-backend feasibility explicit. IMPORTANT distinction verified in source: queryLimits returns LIMITS/ceilings (ProviderRateLimits: requests_per_minute / input_tokens_per_minute / output_tokens_per_minute, types.ts:36-46), which feed discoveredLimits; it does NOT return REMAINING quota. Proactive REMAINING-quota signals belong to a provider-backed QuotaSource (queryCurrentUsage -> QuotaUsageSnapshot) and are composed in the cascade (FINDING-004). Honest feasibility (the reason this finding is confidence=medium): the current backends are CLI/subprocess wrappers with no programmatic quota endpoint, so 'real querying, if possible' resolves to: implement the method, return a real value where a source genuinely exists, otherwise return null with a documented reason and let the cascade fall back. Changes:

(1) packages/shared/src/providers/types.ts: add a JSDoc contract to queryLimits clarifying best-effort semantics — 'Returns provider-reported rate limits when the backend exposes them; returns null (never throws) when no programmatic limit source is available. Callers must treat null as no-signal and fall back through the quota cascade.' No structural shape change required.

(2) packages/audit-code/src/providers/claudeCodeProvider.ts: implement `async queryLimits(_model: string | null): Promise<ProviderRateLimits | null> { return null; }` with a code comment explaining the `claude` CLI exposes no proactive remaining-quota/rate-limit endpoint, so claude-code/claude-desktop rate-limit data is obtained REACTIVELY via the existing claudeCodeHeaderExtractor feeding LearnedQuotaSource/discovered-limits, not proactively here. Keep it total and side-effect-free.

(3) packages/audit-code/src/providers/opencodeProvider.ts: implement queryLimits returning null with an analogous comment (OpenCode brokers arbitrary model backends; no standard proactive quota API). Never throw.

(4) packages/audit-code/src/providers/vscodeTaskProvider.ts: implement queryLimits returning null with an analogous comment (a VS Code task has no quota surface). Never throw.

(5) packages/audit-code/src/quota/probe.ts: it is @deprecated and superseded by the QuotaSource/queryLimits cascade. During implementation, verify probeProvider has no remaining live callers and remove the module if unreferenced; if any caller remains, leave the deprecation in place and do NOT extend it. Do not build new functionality on probe.ts.

Layering: this finding makes FINDING-002's `provider.queryLimits?.(hostModel)` rung functional (it currently no-ops because the method is absent). Genuine proactive querying (issue a minimal request, parse anthropic-ratelimit-* response headers via the header extractors) becomes worthwhile for direct-HTTP-API backends added in FINDING-005 (codex/antigravity); design the null-returning implementations so swapping in a real source later is a drop-in.

tests_to_write.name: each provider implements queryLimits and resolves to null gracefully when no source exists
assertions: ClaudeCodeProvider, OpenCodeProvider, and VSCodeTaskProvider each expose queryLimits as a function (typeof provider.queryLimits === 'function').
queryLimits(null) and queryLimits('claude-opus-4-8') each resolve (no rejection) and return null for the current CLI/subprocess backends.
queryLimits never spawns a process or performs blocking IO that can throw — calling it does not invoke launch.

tests_to_write.name: queryLimits result is consumable by the discovered-limits merge without error
assertions: A null queryLimits result merged via mergeDiscoveredLimits with a cached entry yields the cached entry unchanged (no-signal does not clobber cache).
When queryLimits is wired in FINDING-002, a null return leaves the dispatch quota at its cache/learned/default-derived value (no regression versus today).
